title: Microsoft Defender Attack Surface Reduction (ASR) Rule Triggers from Event Hub
dataSources: Advanced hunting / device events ingested as `sourcetype=ms:defender:eventhub` (Azure Event Hub pipeline in Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security)
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security (Splunkbase 6207), Azure Event Hub
spl:
index=* sourcetype="ms:defender:eventhub" earliest=-24h
| eval action=coalesce(ActionType, action_type, "")
| where like(action, "%Asr%") OR like(lower(action), "%attack surface reduction%")
| eval device=coalesce(DeviceName, deviceName, host, dest)
| stats count by action, device
| sort -count
description: ASR events streamed through Defender advanced hunting into Splunk highlight policy enforcement gaps, noisy rules worth tuning, and hosts repeatedly violating block or audit rules.
value: Operationalizes ASR beyond the Defender portal by correlating rule triggers with asset ownership and change tickets, and by spotting concentrated abuse on a small host population.
implementation: Configure the Event Hub modular input for Microsoft Defender advanced hunting per add-on documentation. Ensure ActionType is extracted; some builds nest fields under JSON—use `spath` if needed. Create lookups for approved ASR exclusions and subtract known-good paths. Start in audit mode baselines before alerting on block noise.
visualization: Bar chart (top ASR ActionType values), table (device × rule), timeline of trigger volume.

references.title: Source types — Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security
